title: The Count of Monte Cristo
author: Alexandre Dumas
content: “All human wisdom is contained in these two words - Wait and Hope.”
action: Exercise extreme patience and prioritize resource accumulation before executing high-stakes maneuvers.

title: The Old Man and the Sea
author: Ernest Hemingway
content: “But man is not made for defeat. A man can be destroyed but not defeated.”
action: Maintain internal resolve even when physical or material resources are depleted.

title: Great Expectations
author: Charles Dickens
content: “I have been bent and broken, but – I hope – into a better shape.”
action: Use periods of failure as an annealing process to increase your future resilience.

title: A Farewell to Arms
author: Ernest Hemingway
content: “The world breaks every one and afterward many are strong at the broken places.”
action: Value systemic trauma as a necessary component of gaining hard operational experience.

title: The Grapes of Wrath
author: John Steinbeck
content: “How can you frighten a man whose hunger is not only in his own cramped stomach but in the wretched bellies of his children?”
action: Identify when external pressures reach an 'unnegotiable' threshold that compels defiance of established rules.
